# Plan: Pane Context Menu Actions

## 1. Overview

Standardize context menu actions (Rename, Delete, Remove) across all five sidebar panes — Collections, Test Lab, Flows, Environments, and History — so every pane has consistent styling (icons, red delete), working delete with a confirmation dialog, rename with uniqueness enforcement, and per-item removal where applicable. This is a UX polish and correctness pass, not new feature work. All changes target `packages/core/` (platform-agnostic) with adapter interface additions for History single-item delete, plus corresponding implementations in `packages/vscode/` and `packages/web/`.

## 2. High-Level Features

| FEAT-ID | Feature | Spec File | Status |
|---------|---------|-----------|--------|
| FEAT-001 | Shared Confirmation Dialog Hook — extract reusable `useConfirmDialog` + `ConfirmDialog` component | `features/feature_shared_confirm_dialog.md` | [x] Complete |
| FEAT-002 | Collections Pane — Menu Icons, Working Delete with Confirmation, Rename | `features/feature_collections_pane_actions.md` | [x] Complete |

> **Note**: The `Spec File` column is populated by the **Feature sub-agent** when it creates each feature spec. The `Status` column is owned exclusively by the **Implement sub-agent** — it marks `[/] In progress` when implementation begins and `[x] Complete` when all tasks are verified.

## 3. Tech Stack Additions

No additions — see memory. All required primitives exist: Radix Dialog, DropdownMenu, Lucide icons, Zustand slices, adapter interfaces.

## 4. Data Model (High-Level)

No new domain entities. Minor additions to existing interfaces only:

### Adapter Interface Changes

- **`IStorageAdapter`** gains `deleteHistoryItem(requestId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>` — needed for FEAT-006 (History single-item removal).

### Store Slice Changes

- **`HistorySlice`** gains `removeHistoryItem(requestId: string): void` — filters the item from global state after adapter confirms deletion.

No other data model changes required. Collections, Environments, Flows, and Test Lab already have the necessary adapter methods (`deleteCollection`, `deleteEnvironment`, `deleteFlow`, `deleteTestSuite`) and store actions (`removeCollection`, `removeEnvironment`, `removeFlow`, `removeTestSuite`).

## 5. Interface Design

All changes are in the sidebar pane UI. No API endpoints, CLI commands, or new screens.

### FEAT-001 — Shared Confirmation Dialog

**New files:**
- `packages/core/src/hooks/useConfirmDialog.tsx` — custom hook returning `{ confirmDialog, openConfirmDialog, ConfirmDialogComponent }`
- Or optionally a `packages/core/src/components/common/ConfirmDialog.tsx` standalone component

**Hook API:**
```ts
interface ConfirmDialogOptions {
  title: string;
  message: string;
  onConfirm: () => void | Promise<void>;
}

function useConfirmDialog(): {
  openConfirmDialog: (options: ConfirmDialogOptions) => void;
  ConfirmDialogComponent: React.FC;
};
```

**Pattern reference:** Current `ValidationStoreGrid.tsx` lines 103–140 (state) and 332–355 (dialog JSX) — uses `Dialog` + `DialogContent` + `SecondaryButton` (Cancel, warningTheme, XIcon) + `PrimaryButton` (Confirm, errorTheme, CheckIcon).

### FEAT-002 — Collections Pane Menus

**Collection-level menu** (in `CollectionsPane.tsx`):
- Run (existing, keep as-is with PlayIcon + green text)
- Rename (new — PencilIcon, inline text input like Test Lab/Flows)
- Delete (existing stub → implement — Trash2Icon, `text-red-600`, confirmation dialog)

**Folder-level menu** (in `CollectionTreeItem.tsx`):
- Run (existing, keep)
- Rename (new — PencilIcon, inline editing)
- Delete (existing stub → implement — Trash2Icon, `text-red-600`, confirmation)

**Request-level menu** (in `CollectionTreeItem.tsx`):
- Rename (new — PencilIcon, inline editing)
- Delete (existing stub → implement — Trash2Icon, `text-red-600`, confirmation)

**Rename uniqueness:** Check sibling items at the same folder level (no duplicates within the same parent). Use in-memory check against the collection tree.

**Delete implementation:**
- Whole collection: call `storageAdapter.deleteCollection(filename)` → `removeCollection(name)` in store
- Folder/request within collection: call `storageAdapter.deleteRequestFromCollection(filename, itemPath, itemId)` → update the collection in store via `updateCollection`

### FEAT-003 — Test Lab Pane

**Remove:** The hover `PlayIcon` run button that appears alongside the dropdown trigger.
**Keep:** Rename + Delete in dropdown menu (already have icons and red styling).
**Add:** Delete confirmation dialog before calling `handleDeleteTestSuite`.

### FEAT-004 — Flows Pane

**Keep:** Run hover button, Rename + Delete in dropdown (already have icons and red styling).
**Add:** Delete confirmation dialog before calling `handleDeleteFlow`.

### FEAT-005 — Environments Pane

**Add DropdownMenu** to each environment row (on hover, like other panes):
- Rename (PencilIcon — inline text input)
- Delete (Trash2Icon, `text-red-600`, confirmation dialog)

**Rename:** `updateEnvironment(id, { name: trimmedName })` + `storageAdapter.saveEnvironment(updatedEnv)`. Uniqueness check against existing environment names (case-insensitive, like `addEnvironment` already does).

**Delete:** `storageAdapter.deleteEnvironment(id)` → `removeEnvironment(id)` in store. Already wired in adapter.

### FEAT-006 — History Pane

**Add per-item remove button** (on hover, right side of each history row):
- `XIcon` or `Trash2Icon` (`h-3 w-3`, `text-red-600`)
- No confirmation dialog needed (history items are ephemeral / easily re-created)

**New adapter method:** `deleteHistoryItem(requestId: string)` — removes a single entry from persisted history.
**New store action:** `removeHistoryItem(requestId: string)` — filters item from `history` array.

**Implementation in adapters:**
- `webAdapter.ts`: filter from IndexedDB/localStorage history array, persist
- `vsCodeAdapter.ts`: postMessage `{ type: 'deleteHistoryItem', payload: { requestId } }`
- `packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts`: handle `deleteHistoryItem` message type, delegate to `HistoryService`

## 6. Security Considerations

No plan-specific concerns — see memory. All operations use the existing adapter boundary. No new external input surfaces.

## 7. Architecture & Design (Plan-Specific)

### Plan-Specific Decisions

- **Shared `useConfirmDialog` hook over duplicated state**: Five panes need identical confirmation dialog behavior. Extracting a hook follows the Custom Hook Extraction pattern from memory and avoids duplicating ~30 lines of state + JSX in every pane.
- **Collection rename uses `saveCollection` round-trip**: Rather than adding a dedicated `renameCollectionItem` adapter method, rename mutates the item name in the in-memory collection tree and calls `saveCollection` to persist the whole file. This reuses the existing adapter surface and keeps the change minimal.
- **History item removal without confirmation**: History entries are low-value ephemeral records (re-created on every request send). A confirmation dialog would be friction without benefit. A simple hover-to-remove pattern (like closing a tab) is appropriate.

## 8. Coding Standards (Plan-Specific Overrides)

No overrides — see memory.

## 9. Testing Strategy (Plan-Specific)

- **FEAT-001**: Unit test `useConfirmDialog` hook — open/close/confirm lifecycle, callback invocation.
- **FEAT-002**: Component tests for `CollectionTreeItem` and `CollectionsPane` — verify Rename inline editing, uniqueness error, Delete confirmation flow (dialog appears → confirm → adapter called → store updated), menu icon rendering.
- **FEAT-003**: Component test for `TestLabPane` — verify Run hover button is removed, Delete shows confirmation before executing.
- **FEAT-004**: Component test for `FlowsPane` — verify Delete shows confirmation before executing.
- **FEAT-005**: Component test for `EnvironmentsPane` — verify dropdown menu appears, Rename works with uniqueness check, Delete with confirmation.
- **FEAT-006**: Component test for `HistoryPane` — verify remove button appears on hover, clicking it calls adapter and removes from list. Unit test for `removeHistoryItem` store action.

All tests use `createMockAdapter()` and `<AdapterProvider>` wrapper per memory conventions.

## 10. Logging Strategy (Plan-Specific)

No additions — see memory. Standard error logging on adapter call failures (already enforced by error handling conventions).
